volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: Pseudolus (Section 4.1.954 - Section 4.2.1016b)

sections.label: Section 4.1.954
sections.translation: Is this he ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.954b
sections.translation: This is his own self .

sections.label: Section 4.1.954c
sections.translation: ’ Tis a worthless commodity .

sections.label: Section 4.1.954d
sections.translation: Do see that : he doesn ’ t go straight , but sideways , just as a crab is wont .

sections.label: Section 4.2.956
sections.original: Mínus malum hunc hominem esse opinor quam esse censebam coquom ,
sections.translation: I do believe that this fellow is not so bad a cook as I thought he was ; for he has clawed off nothing as yet except a cup and a tankard .

sections.label: Section 4.2.958
sections.original: Heus tu , nunc occasio est et tempus .
sections.translation: Hallo you ! now ’ s your opportunity and your time .

sections.label: Section 4.2.958b
sections.translation: I agree with you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.959
sections.original: Ingredere in viam dolose : ego hic in insidiis ero .
sections.translation: Step slily out into the street ; I ’ ll be here in ambush .

sections.label: Section 4.2.960
sections.original: Habui numerum sedulo : hoc est sextum a porta proxumum
sections.translation: I took the number carefully ; this is the sixth lane from the city gate ; down that lane he bade me turn ; how many houses down he told me , that I don ’ t quite know for certain .

sections.label: Section 4.2.963
sections.original: Quis hic homo chlamydatus est aut unde est aut quem quaeritat ?
sections.translation: Who ’ s this fellow in the scarf , or whence does he come , or whom is he looking for ? The appearance of the fellow seems outlandish and shabby .

sections.label: Section 4.2.965
sections.original: Sed eccum qui ex incerto faciet mihi quod quaero certius .
sections.translation: But see , here ’ s a person , who , from uncertainty , will make the thing more certain for me that I wish to know .

sections.label: Section 4.2.966
sections.original: Ad me adit recta . unde ego hominem hunc esse dicam gentium ?
sections.translation: He ’ s coming straight towards me . Where in the world am I to say this fellow comes from ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.967
sections.original: Heus tu , qui cum hirquina barba stas , responde quod rogo .
sections.translation: Harkye ! you who are standing there with a goat ’ s beard , answer me this that I ask you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.968
sections.translation: How now ! Don ’ t you salute me first ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.968b
sections.translation: I have no salutations to give away .

sections.label: Section 4.2.969_970
sections.translation: Well , troth , you shall get just as much from here then .

sections.label: Section 4.2.969_970b
sections.translation: Well done , at the very beginning .

sections.label: Section 4.2.971
sections.original: Ecquem in angiporto hoc hominem tu novisti ? te rogo .
sections.translation: Do you know any person in this lane , I ask you ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.972
sections.translation: I know myself .

sections.label: Section 4.2.972b
sections.original: Pauci istuc faciunt homines quod tu praedicas ,
sections.translation: Few persons do that which you mention ; for in the Forum there is hardly every tenth person that knows his own self .

sections.label: Section 4.2.974
sections.translation: I ’ m all right ; he is philosophizing now .

sections.label: Section 4.2.974b
sections.translation: I ’ m looking for a fellow here , a bad one ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.975
sections.translation: a law breaker , an impious , perjured , and dishonest rogue .

sections.label: Section 4.2.975b
sections.translation: He ’ s looking for me , for those are my titles If he would only mention the name . ( To SIMMIA .) What ’ s the name of this person ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.977b
sections.translation: Ballio , the procurer .

sections.label: Section 4.2.977c
sections.translation: Do I know him ? I am the very person , young man , that you are looking for .

sections.label: Section 4.2.978b
sections.translation: What , are you Ballio ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.979_980
sections.translation: I really am he .

sections.label: Section 4.2.979_980b
sections.original: Vt vestitu ’ (s e) s , es perfossor parietum .
sections.translation: How you are clothed , a housebreaker ⋯

sections.label: Section 4.2.981
sections.original: Credo , in tenebris , conspicatus si sis me , apstineas manum .
sections.translation: I think if you were to see me in the dark , you ’ d be keeping your hand off .

sections.label: Section 4.2.982
sections.original: Erus meus tibi me salutem multam voluit dicere .
sections.translation: My master bade me present you many greetings . Receive this letter from me ; he bade me give you it .

sections.label: Section 4.2.984
sections.translation: Who ’ s the person that bade you ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.984b
sections.original: Perii , nunc homo in medio lutost ;
sections.translation: I ’ m undone , now the fellow ’ s in the middle of the mud .

sections.label: Section 4.2.985
sections.translation: He doesn ’ t know the name — this business is at a dead lock .

sections.label: Section 4.2.985b
sections.original: Quem hanc misisse ad me autumas ?
sections.translation: Who do you say sent me this ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.986
sections.original: Nosce imaginem : tute eius nomen memorato mihi ,
sections.translation: Observe the seal ; do you yourself tell me his name , that I may know that you are Ballio himself .

sections.label: Section 4.2.987b
sections.translation: Give me the letter .

sections.label: Section 4.2.988
sections.translation: Take it , and look at the seal .

sections.label: Section 4.2.988b
sections.translation: Oho ! ’ Tis nothing more nor less than Polymachaeroplagides [*] , his own very self ; I recognize it . Hallo you , Polymachaeroplagides

sections.label: Section 4.2.990
sections.translation: is his name !

sections.label: Section 4.2.990b
sections.original: Scio iám me recte tibi dedisse epistulam ,
sections.translation: I know now that I have rightly given you the letter , since you have mentioned the name of Polymachaeroplagides ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.992
sections.translation: How fares he ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.992b
sections.original: Quod homo edepol fortis atque bellator probus .
sections.translation: By my troth , just as a brave man and a good soldier should . But make haste , I beg , to read this letter through , for it is requisite to do so , and to take this money at once and send out the damsel .

sections.label: Section 4.2.995
sections.original: nam necessést hodie Sicyoni me esse aut cras mortem exsequi ,
sections.translation: For it ’ s necessary for me this day to be at Sicyon [*] , or else to suffer death to-morrow ; so peremptory is my master .

sections.label: Section 4.2.996b
sections.translation: I know it : you are telling those who know it already .

sections.label: Section 4.2.997
sections.translation: Make haste then to read the letter through .

sections.label: Section 4.2.997b
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so , if you ’ ll only hold your tongue . ( He reads the letter .) The Captain Polymachaeroplagides sends this letter , written to the procurer Ballio ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.1000
sections.original: imagine obsignatam quae inter nos duo
sections.translation: sealed with the impression which was formerly agreed upon between us two . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.2.1001
sections.translation: The token ’ s in the letter

sections.label: Section 4.2.1002
sections.original: video , et cognosco signum . sed in epistula
sections.translation: I see the token and I recognize it . But is he in the habit of sending no greeting written in his letter ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1004
sections.translation: Such is the military etiquette , Ballio ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.1005
sections.translation: with their hand they send health to their well-wishers , and with the same do they send destruction to their evil-wishers . But as you have commenced , go on to ascertain of yourself what this letter says .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1008b
sections.translation: Listen then . ( Reading on .) Harpax is my camp-servant , who has come to you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1010
sections.translation: Are you this Harpax ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1010b
sections.original: Ego sum , atque ipse ἅρπαξ quidem .
sections.translation: I am , and the real Harpax too [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1011
sections.original: “ Qui epistulam istam fert ; ab eo argentum accipi ,
sections.translation: Who brings this letter ; I wish the money to be received from him , and the woman to be sent together with him . ’ Tis becoming to send greeting to the worthy ; had I deemed you worthy , I should have sent it to you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1015
sections.translation: What then ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1015b
sections.translation: Pay me the money , take away the woman .

sections.label: Section 4.2.1016
sections.translation: Which of us is delaying the matter ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.1016b
sections.translation: Follow me in-doors then .
